Test Description: Checks GPIO register default values and verifies write/read behavior across a defined register list using per-register read/write masks.
Remarks: - VRRW registers are skipped per skip_array comment. - Some default-value reads affected by DIN behavior: if no force, DIN becomes 1; forcing 0 may drive bit-level select high causing mismatch (per source comment).
Test Steps / Procedure: 1) test_case entry. 2) Call chk_rst_val: For i = 0..(CNT-1): a) addr = addr_array[i]. b) If skip_rst_array[i] == 1: continue. c) If read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: continue. d) data_rd = read_reg(addr). e) data = (data_rd & 0xFFFFFFFE). f) If data == default_value_array[i]: no action; else def_fail_cnt++. 3) Call chk_rd_wr: For each data pattern in [0xFFFFFFFF, 0xAAAAAAAA, 0x55555555, 0xF5F5F5F5, 0xA5A5A5A5, 0xFFFF0000]: a) data_wr = pattern. b) Write phase, for i = 0..(CNT-1): i1) addr = addr_array[i]. i2) If skip_array[i] == 1: continue. i3) If write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: continue. i4) write_reg(addr, (data_wr & write_mask_array[i])). c) Read/compare phase, for i = 0..(CNT-1): ii1) addr = addr_array[i]. ii2) If skip_array[i] == 1: continue. ii3) If write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: continue. ii4) If read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: continue. ii5) data_rd = (read_reg(addr) & read_mask_array[i]). ii6) wr_n = (write_mask_array[i] ^ 0xFFFFFFFF). ii7) exp_val = ((data_wr & read_mask_array[i] & write_mask_array[i]) | (wr_n & read_mask_array[i] & default_value_array[i])). ii8) If data_rd == exp_val: no action; else wr_fail_cnt++. 4) If (def_fail_cnt > 0 || wr_fail_cnt > 0): finish(1); else finish(0).
Validation / Acceptance Criteria: Pass when both counters remain zero and finish(0) is invoked: - def_fail_cnt == 0 for default value checks. - wr_fail_cnt == 0 for all write/read pattern checks.
Gap Analysis: No negative tests; no explicit error handling paths; soft_reset_chk is compiled out (#ifdef 0).

Test Description: Validates GPIO group interrupt behavior on negative edge events across pads 8-39, including enable, trigger via external driver address, ISR handling, raw/group status clear, and system register clearing.
Test Steps / Procedure: 1) test_case entry; test_err = 0. 2) If GPIO0 defined: GIC_EnableIRQ(87); If GPIO1 defined: GIC_EnableIRQ(88). 3) If GPIO0 defined: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR); If GPIO1 defined: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR). 4) Drive external control register: write_reg(0xA0243FFC, 0xFFFFFFFF). 5) For i = 0..31: a) addr1 = MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4). b) write_reg(addr1, (1<<20) | (1<<18) | (1<<16)). c) wait_on(10). 6) For i = 0..31: a) wr_val = (1<<i). b) write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, wr_val). c) write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, wr_val). d) wait_on(10). e) int_pend = 1. f) write_reg(0xA0243FFC, 0xFFFFFFFF). g) wait_on(30). h) write_reg(0xA0243FFC, ~wr_val). i) timeout = 5000; while (int_pend && timeout--) wait_on(10). j) If timeout == 0: printf timeout error; test_err++. 7) finish(test_err). 8) Default_IRQHandler: a) local_wr = (1<<i). b) int_pend = 0. c) write_reg(0xA0243FFC, 0xFFFFFFFF). d) raddr = MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4); rdata = read_reg(raddr). e) If (rdata & 0x1) != 0: test_err++. f) If (rdata & 0x2) != 0x0: i) rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1). ii) If ((rdata_grp & local_wr) == 0): test_err++. iii) write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4), (1<<20) | (1<<16)). iv) write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, local_wr). v) rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); If (rdata_grp != 0x0): test_err++. vi) If GPIO0 defined: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR); GIC_ClearIRQ(87). vii) If GPIO1 defined: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR); GIC_ClearIRQ(88). g) Else: test_err++.
Validation / Acceptance Criteria: Pass when: - No timeout occurs while waiting for ISR (int_pend cleared before timeout). - In ISR: (rdata & 0x1) == 0; (rdata & 0x2) != 0x0. - Group status includes local_wr bit before clear and becomes 0 after clear. - System register RAW_STCR1 bit for the active GPIOx is cleared after write. - finish(test_err) called with test_err == 0.
Gap Analysis: No negative tests; limited error handling (timeout increments counter only); dependency on external 0xA0243FFC drive not validated within test.

Test Description: Enables and verifies GPIO group interrupt behavior on positive edges for pads 8-39, including configuration, per-pin raw clear, group status clear, and sysreg status clear via ISR.
Test Steps / Procedure: 1) test_case entry. 2) If GPIO0 defined: GIC_EnableIRQ(87); If GPIO1 defined: GIC_EnableIRQ(88). 3) If GPIO0 defined: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR); If GPIO1 defined: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR). 4) For i = 0..31: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4), 0x00020000). 5) wait_on(10). 6) Configure IO control: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP1, 0x000000FF); GROUP2=0x000000FF; GROUP3=0x000000FF; GROUP4=0x000000FF. 7) wait_on(10). 8) Enable group interrupt: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0xFFFFFFFF). 9) For i = 0..31: a) write_reg(0xA0243FFC, 0x00000000). b) wait_on(10). c) int_pend = 1. d) write_reg(0xA0243FFC, 0xFFFFFFFF). e) timeout = 2000; while (int_pend == 1 && --timeout > 0) wait_on(10). f) If timeout == 0: printf timeout error; test_err++; break. g) write_reg(0xA0243FFC, 0x00000000). h) wait_on(10). 10) finish(test_err). 11) Default_IRQHandler: a) wr_val = (1<<i). b) int_pend = 0. c) rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1). d) write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0x00000000). e) If (rdata_grp & 0xFFFFFFFF) != 0: success; else: printf error; test_err++. f) For j = 0..31: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (j*4), 0x00010000). g) wait_on(2). h) rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); If (rdata_grp == 0x0): success; else: printf error; test_err++. i) If GPIO0 defined: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR); verify bit cleared; If GPIO1 defined: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR); verify bit cleared; If bit remains set: test_err++. j) write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0xFFFFFFFF). k) GIC_ClearIRQ(87 or 88 per macro).
Validation / Acceptance Criteria: Pass when: - No timeout occurs while waiting for ISR (int_pend cleared before timeout). - In ISR: group interrupt status is non-zero before per-pin clear, becomes 0 after clearing raw per-pin status. - System register RAW_STCR1 bit for the active GPIOx is cleared after write. - finish(test_err) called with test_err == 0.
Gap Analysis: No negative tests; relies on external drive via 0xA0243FFC; minimal error handling (timeouts and simple counter increments).
